purpose: Decompose Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) into 6.A.1 / 6.A.2 / 6.B sub-phases mirroring the established X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B pattern from Phases 3 / 4 / 5. Phase 5 was end-to-end verified by phase-5.B (CI run 25328387560 green: deploy + 54 cross-runtime Playwright assertions + cross-OS atlas matrix); Phase 6 is unblocked.
trigger: Phase 5.B was approved at HEAD of commit a9162a5 on 2026-05-04 with all gates green (deploy workflow success in 6m 55s; 18 e2e tests × 3 browsers = 54 assertions; cross-OS matrix byte-identical across ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest). The next unapproved phase per docs/PHASES.md is Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment). Phase 6 is comparable in surface area to Phase 4.A.2 — substantial new sim work (inventory + equipment data shape + 5 new action-vocabulary types + tick handling), substantial new content (~20 starter items, ~20 atlas recipes), substantial new UI (inventory + equipment screens). The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition that Phases 3 / 4 / 5 used is the right structural fit: a small drift-detection sweep + ARCHITECTURE.md update before the implementation lands, the implementation in a single sandbox-verifiable commit, then live-deploy verification post-push. Phase 6 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7 (planning gates are 1.A, 2, 3, 4, 8 only), so the decomposition is recorded in this phase-update artifact rather than a decision memo. Material design choices (inventory shape, equipment slot enumeration, action vocabulary schema) are resolved during Phase 6.A.1's drift sweep via a brief strategy-planner pass if multiple plausible options surface; the output lives in the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 6 section.

phase_5_b_post_approval_summary.head_commit: a12ef14 (phase-5.A.2; phase-5.B observational only — no source changes beyond docs/PHASES.md callout + phase-update.json which were committed in 92ccfb8 + a9162a5)
phase_5_b_post_approval_summary.jobs_all_green: build & test (1m 56s including 54 cross-runtime Playwright assertions); cross-os atlas equality (ubuntu-latest 24s + macos-latest 19s + windows-latest 1m 10s + aggregate 4s, all byte-identical at d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a); deploy to GitHub Pages 4m 43s
phase_5_b_post_approval_summary.verdict: Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) is complete and end-to-end verified. The renderer-on-sim-state read-only contract holds; the diagnostic surface preservation rule holds; the atlas determinism continues to hold across all three OSes. No phase-update fix-up required.

phase_6_split_decision.rationale: Phase 6 introduces inventory + equipment as net-new sim mechanics, ~20 starter items + ~20 atlas recipes (atlas binary regenerates → cross-OS matrix re-runs against a new binary, not just continued passing), 5 new action-vocabulary types (pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use additive to the Phase 1 schema), and inventory + equipment UI screens. This crosses sim, atlas, and ui layers and bumps the ATLAS_DIGEST + preset-seed expectedHash goldens. The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition is the right structural fit and the established pattern. Phase 6 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7, so the decomposition is recorded here rather than via a decision-memo + architecture-red-team cycle. Material design choices that surface during Phase 6.A.1's drift sweep (inventory shape, equipment slot enumeration, action vocabulary schema, item-effect resolution path) are resolved via a brief strategy-planner pass if needed, with output in the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 6 section.

phase_6_A_1.name: drift-detection sweep
phase_6_A_1.scope: Address Phase 5.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards from artifacts/code-review-phase-5-A-2.md where they touch files Phase 6.A.2 will modify (specifically the Direction cast helper if Phase 6.A.2 extends DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS with inventory keys, and the fillStyle = '#000' hidden constant if Phase 6.A.2 extends the renderer with inventory tile drawing); address Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards now load-bearing because Phase 6.A.2 modifies sim files (applyFloorEntry redundant param at run.ts since Phase 6.A.2 modifies run.ts to initialize inventory; tick unknown-action defensive type-check at turn.ts since Phase 6.A.2 extends the action vocabulary; ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant at combat.ts if Phase 6.A.2 extends the roll-domain registry for item-effect rolls); update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with Phase 6 frozen contracts (inventory data shape with deterministic ordering — sorted by slot then by ItemKindId; equipment slot enumeration — weapon, cyberware, consumable, currency, etc.; the additive action vocabulary additions for pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use with their byte-explicit wire format per the Phase 1 frozen contract; item-effect resolution path through the existing per-action roll subhash with new domain anchors like 'item:effect:atk-bonus' / 'item:effect:def-bonus'; registry append-only invariant for items with the existing test deferred-to-Phase-6 from Phase 2 decision memo finally landing as a regression-failing test). NO net-new inventory / equipment / item-recipe code lands in 6.A.1.
phase_6_A_1.expected_diff_size: small — primarily docs/ARCHITECTURE.md additions + a few cosmetic source patches + the long-deferred registry-immutability enforcement test from Phase 2 decision memo decision 6.

phase_6_A_2.name: sandbox-verifiable implementation
phase_6_A_2.scope: Inventory + equipment types in src/sim/types.ts (deterministic ordering: inventory sorted by ItemKindId; equipment as a fixed-slot record); 5 new Action descriptors in src/core/encode.ts additive to the Phase 1 schema (new type strings: pickup, drop, equip, unequip, use; new optional fields with strictly-greater tags than the existing TAG_TARGET=0x10 / TAG_ITEM=0x20 / TAG_DIR=0x30 — likely TAG_INVENTORY_INDEX=0x40); tick() extended in src/sim/turn.ts to handle the 5 new actions with deterministic effect resolution (pickup removes a FloorItem from the floor and adds it to RunState.player.inventory; drop is the inverse; equip moves an inventory item to the equipment slot; unequip is the inverse; use consumes a consumable and applies its effect via the existing rollU32 path); item registry expanded to ~20 starter items across weapons / cyberware / consumables / currency categories in src/registries/items.ts (Phase 3 had item.cred-chip only; Phase 6 adds ~19 more); ~20 atlas recipes added under src/atlas/recipes/ (registry append-only per addendum 3a coordinate-stability invariant; existing 7 sprite coordinates remain unchanged); assets/atlas.png regenerated via npm run gen-atlas — atlas grid now uses ~27 cells (still under the 128-cell budget); ATLAS_DIGEST golden bumped in src/core/self-test.ts; four preset-seed expectedHash values bumped in src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts; new SIM_DIGEST extended via a new SELF_TEST_LOG that exercises the inventory loop (pickup → equip → attack → assert hash) OR a new INVENTORY_DIGEST golden; inventory + equipment UI screens in new src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts (read-only on RunState per Phase 5 contract); inventory-from-log reconstruction test asserting replay(actions) === RunState byte-equality.
phase_6_A_2.expected_diff_size: large — comparable to Phase 4.A.2's surface area (5 new actions + tick handlers + 20 items + 20 atlas recipes + new UI modules + atlas regeneration + multiple golden bumps + extensive new tests). Bundle size impact: each atlas recipe is ~30-50 lines of TypeScript compiled to the encoder pipeline; the JS bundle should grow by ~10-15 KB raw / ~3-5 KB gzipped, staying well under the 75 KB budget.

phase_6_B.name: live-deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS verification
phase_6_B.scope: Verify the live GitHub Pages URL serves the updated playable game with inventory UI; verify cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit exercises the pickup → equip → kill scripted sequence on a fixed seed and asserts the final state hash matches a pinned golden hex value; verify the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-runs against the new atlas binary and reports green on all three OSes (the new atlas is larger by ~13 sprites but the deterministic-encoder discipline should hold; drift on macOS or Windows is a Phase 6.B blocker requiring a phase-update). Phase 6 is complete when 6.A.1, 6.A.2, and 6.B are all approved.
phase_6_B.expected_diff_size: minimal — observational verification + maybe one new Playwright test extension. The X.B verification commit's CI assertions only fire on the next push of the X.B commit itself per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B chicken-and-egg pattern.

phase_6_split_decision.docs_changes: docs/PHASES.md updated with the Phase 6 split callout block (mirroring the Phase 3 lines 131-148, Phase 4 lines 191-224, and Phase 5 lines 286-316 callouts) and the partitioned acceptance criteria for 6.A.1 / 6.A.2 / 6.B. No other docs changes required for this phase-update.

next_phase: Phase 6.A.1 — drift-detection sweep per the acceptance criteria recorded in docs/PHASES.md. The drift sweep should land the long-deferred Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards that touch files Phase 6.A.2 will modify (run.ts applyFloorEntry redundant param, turn.ts tick unknown-action defensive type-check, combat.ts ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES shared constant), the Phase 5.A.2 carry-forwards that touch files Phase 6.A.2 will modify, and the Phase 6 frozen-contract additions to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. Material design choices (inventory shape, equipment slot enumeration, action vocabulary schema, item-effect resolution path) may benefit from a brief strategy-planner pass during 6.A.1 if multiple plausible options surface; output lives in the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 6 section. The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-6.A.1 from the docs/PHASES.md acceptance criteria.
